reasoning:
The question asks whether there will be ANY 'Battles' (defined by ACLED as violent interactions between two organized armed groups) in the 30 days prior to four resolution dates (2025-06-01, 2025-06-24, 2025-08-23, and 2025-11-21) compared to the preceding 360-day baseline. The baseline is currently exactly 0.00 battles. For this condition to be met, at least one 'Battle' must be recorded in North Macedonia within the 30-day window for each specific date.

North Macedonia has not experienced any events categorized as 'Battles' since at least 2015 (the Kumanovo clashes). The baseline implies no battles occurred in the 360 days leading up to May 2025. Extensive searches for any signs of armed insurgencies, developing conflicts, armed group shootouts, border clashes, or political crises escalating to violence in 2024–2025 yielded no evidence. Occasional protests occur, but ACLED defines Protests (non-violent by participants) and Riots (violent mobs, rock throwing) separately from Battles (organized armed groups fighting). Furthermore, the ACLED definition of Battle requires two organized armed groups; in North Macedonia, such groups (e.g., ethnic Albanian insurgents) have been largely inactive or dismantled for a decade. The likelihood of a sudden, unforeseeable resurgence of an organized armed group engaging in a shootout with security forces within a narrow 30-day window is incredibly low, close to 0. 

I set the probability to the minimum 0.02 for the near-term dates and 0.03 for the dates further out (August and November), to reflect increasing uncertainty over longer time horizons, acknowledging the tiny but non-zero possibility of a sudden, unexpected incident (e.g. an organized cross-border smuggling ring shootout that ACLED classifies as a battle).
